questions.question: What does the print() function do in Python?
options: Displays text or values to the screen
questions.explanation: print() outputs text or values to the console so you can see what your program is doing.

questions.question: Complete the code to display Hello on screen: _____('Hello')
Who created Python?
questions.explanation: Guido van Rossum created Python in 1991. The language was named after Monty Python's Flying Circus.

questions.question: Python is a ___________ language.
questions.explanation: Python is interpreted — code runs directly without a separate compilation step.

questions.question: Is Python case-sensitive? Type yes or no: _____
What file extension do Python files use?
questions.explanation: Python source files use the .py extension.
